refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR uses 'support achieving' which is somewhat vague and potentially implies a solution approach. Replacing 'support achieving' with 'enable achieving' keeps the focus on the system's functional capability without prescribing how. The FR is atomic as it addresses a single functional objective: achieving the energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the energy_per_ton metric (energy_used divided by ton_moved) over the agreed evaluation period and verify it meets or is less than the customer-defined target E_target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control
Optimize operational parameters to minimize energy per ton moved
Incorporate adaptive load management to balance energy use and throughput
Design energy-efficient mechanical and electrical subsystems
Enable user-configurable energy efficiency targets with automated adjustment
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control

potential_sources: Tight integration required between energy monitoring and control subsystems
Dependency on accurate load and throughput measurement
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates the energy efficiency control function, enabling modular implementation and minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and timely measurement of energy used and ton moved is feasible
Operational policies allow for automated control adjustments
Energy efficiency targets are stable or change slowly enough for control adaptation
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including measurement and control subsystems as separate DPs if complexity grows, to maintain modularity and reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for energy management and monitoring within the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time data on energy consumption and system status for monitoring and feedback control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Enables communication with other system modules and external services for control commands and data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as energy monitoring and feedback control logically belong here; minimal diffusion as most related functions are internal.
Contains physical energy consumption but lacks direct control and monitoring logic, causing moderate diffusion.
Primarily focused on remote control, not energy monitoring; high diffusion if included here.
May use energy data for safety alerts but not responsible for monitoring/control; moderate diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control, as it centralizes energy management functions and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry interface reliability for real-time data.
Possible overlap with Vehicle module if physical energy sensors are tightly integrated, requiring clear interface definitions.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target by enabling measurement and control to meet the energy_per_ton ≤ E_target acceptance criterion.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module due to physical energy sensors integration, which may cause diffusion if interfaces are not clearly defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well contained within a single module responsible for energy management and monitoring. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through telemetry data providing real-time energy consumption and system status, enabling measurement of energy_per_ton against the target over the evaluation period.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, with well-defined external interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) supporting data exchange and control commands.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and formalize the interface definitions between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules to manage sensor data integration and avoid diffusion.
Establish reliability requirements and fallback strategies for the Telemetry interface to mitigate dependency risks.
Document the boundary responsibilities explicitly to prevent inadvertent diffusion into Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The NGHE Solution module shows high containment and low diffusion, and verification feasibility is confirmed. Although some interface clarifications and risk mitigations are recommended, these do not necessitate further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to finalize this level before proceeding further.

revise.why: Clarify and formalize interface definitions between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules to manage sensor data integration and avoid diffusion; establish reliability requirements and fallback strategies for the Telemetry interface; document boundary responsibilities explicitly to prevent inadvertent diffusion into other modules.

assumptions: The current DP fully satisfies the FR with measurable acceptance criteria.
The NGHE Solution module can be reliably implemented as specified.
Telemetry data and network links provide sufficient verification capability.
open_risks: Potential interface diffusion between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules if interfaces are not clearly defined.
Dependency risks on Telemetry interface reliability.
Possible inadvertent diffusion into Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.
evidence_needed: Formal interface specifications between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.
Reliability and fallback strategy documentation for Telemetry interface.
Boundary responsibility documentation for involved modules.
